title: jurinea pumila
intro: Jurinea pumila, also known as the dwarf jurinea, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Its roots and rhizomes have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Jurinea pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of Jurinea pumila can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
